hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while avoiding off-target complementarity. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 selectivity, but structural issues are limiting the number of viable candidates. Tightening structural constraints and increasing candidate diversity should improve the overall yield of high-quality, selective designs.

proposed_changes.rationale: Increase the number of candidates to improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs that pass the QC gate.
Tighten the clash threshold to reduce structural failures due to steric clashes, especially in the binding pocket.
